frameworks.name: neural correlates of consciousness (NCC)
frameworks.one_line: Brain features whose activity sustains perceptual conscious experience.

frameworks.name: hierarchical processing architecture
frameworks.one_line: Cortical hierarchy in which prefrontal cortex plays a crucial role.

frameworks.name: posterior / sensory-only accounts of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: Views that minimize prefrontal contribution to conscious perception.

core_question: Which neural features are critical for sustaining perceptual conscious experience in humans and other primates?
key_assumptions: Perceptual consciousness has identifiable neural correlates
Imaging and computational modelling can adjudicate between competing NCC accounts
Some hypotheses can be empirically ruled out
key_findings: New imaging and statistical techniques have advanced NCC research
Several prior NCC hypotheses have been conclusively ruled out
Neuroscientists still disagree on the critical features for conscious perception
interpretations: Current evidence favors a hierarchical processing architecture
Prefrontal cortex plays a crucial, subtle, and specific role in conscious perception
limitations_noted: No full consensus among neuroscientists on critical NCC features
